summary: Matched two previously handwritten assembly functions in src/sysdolphin/id.c: HSD_IDGetDataFromTable and _HSD_IDForgetMemory. The PR replaced inline asm with C, preserving behavior for default table fallback, hash bucket lookup, success out-parameter handling, and global ID table clearing.
what_changed: Converted HSD_IDGetDataFromTable from inline PowerPC assembly to C.
Converted _HSD_IDForgetMemory from inline PowerPC assembly to C.
Added #pragma peephole on around the newly matched C functions.
Implemented HSD_IDGetDataFromTable by falling back to global table lbl_804C23EC when the table argument is NULL.
Implemented hash bucket traversal using entry = (&table->table[0].next)[hash(id)] followed by walking IDEntry::next.
Set *success to 1 when a matching IDEntry is found and success is non-NULL.
Set *success to 0 when no matching entry is found and success is non-NULL.
Returned entry->data on match and NULL on miss.
smart_moves: Used the existing hash(id) helper instead of spelling out the compiler's modulo-by-101 multiply/shift sequence from assembly.
Preserved the original NULL-table behavior by redirecting to lbl_804C23EC before indexing the table.
Matched the original success out-parameter semantics exactly: only write when success is non-NULL, write 1 on hit and 0 on miss.
Used pointer arithmetic on &table->table[0].next to reproduce the original bucket load shape.
Reduced _HSD_IDForgetMemory to the semantic memset call, matching the original call to memset with size 0x194.
Enabled peephole optimization locally, indicating the match depended on CodeWarrior peephole behavior for these functions.
decomp_lessons: For ID table lookups, the assembly hash arithmetic can often be represented by an existing hash(id) helper, but final matching may depend on indexing expression shape.
When matching linked-list bucket traversals, preserve the exact order of checks and updates: load bucket, loop while non-NULL, compare id, return data on hit, otherwise advance to next.
Out-parameters should mirror assembly control flow: guard writes with NULL checks and avoid writing on early paths unless the original code did.
Simple global reset routines may match cleanly as memset calls if the address, fill value, and byte count are exact.
Local #pragma peephole on can be a useful matching tactic when replacing inline asm with C in sysdolphin code.
naming_conventions: Function names retained existing HSD/sysdolphin naming: HSD_IDGetDataFromTable and _HSD_IDForgetMemory.
The matched code uses IDEntry for ID table nodes.
The global/default ID table symbol remains lbl_804C23EC because the PR evidence does not rename or identify it further.
The output flag is named success and is treated as an optional s32 pointer.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced nofralloc inline asm blocks with C while preserving function signatures.
Used #pragma push / #pragma pop to scope compiler pragmas.
Enabled #pragma peephole on for both matched functions.
Kept #pragma force_active on for _HSD_IDForgetMemory, likely to preserve symbol emission/linkage behavior.
Represented the original bucket selection arithmetic through hash(id).
Represented the original memset call in _HSD_IDForgetMemory directly as memset(&lbl_804C23EC, 0, 0x194).
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or type lbl_804C23EC as a concrete HSD ID table global.
Search for other uses of hash(id) in sysdolphin/id.c to compare bucket indexing patterns.
Check later cleanup around HSD_IDTable and IDEntry struct definitions for whether (&table->table[0].next)[hash(id)] was replaced with a clearer field access.
Look for additional PRs that use #pragma peephole on to match small sysdolphin functions.
